rule.description: Suricata IDS: $(alert.signature)
DoH DNS Exfiltration: HTTPS connection to DoH server detected
DoH DNS Exfiltration CAMPAIGN: repeated connections to DoH server
Suricata IDS CRITICAL: $(alert.signature)
Suricata IDS HIGH: $(alert.signature)
SSH brute force attack (8+ failures in 2 min) from $(srcip)
Successful login after brute force from $(srcip) — possible credential compromise
Directory traversal attempt from $(srcip)
BIND DNS query from $(srcip): $(query_name) $(query_type)
Possible DNS reconnaissance: 50+ queries in 60s from $(srcip)
DNS zone transfer attempt: $(srcip) requested $(query_type) $(query_name)
AD service-record enumeration from $(srcip): $(query_name)
Falco event detected
Falco CRITICAL on $(hostname): $(output_fields.proc.cmdline)
Falco WARNING on $(hostname): $(output_fields.proc.cmdline)

rule.match: Reverse shell|Netcat Remote Code|terminal shell in
rule.description: Falco: shell/netcat activity on $(hostname) - $(output_fields.proc.cmdline)
Falco: sensitive file read on $(hostname) - $(output_fields.fd.name) by $(output_fields.proc.name)
Falco: shell from service process on $(hostname) - parent=$(output_fields.proc.pname) cmd=$(output_fields.proc.cmdline)
PAM session opened (srv-dns-bind: suppressed routine)
PAM session closed (srv-dns-bind: suppressed routine)
Successful sudo (srv-dns-bind: routine from mgmt)
BIND configuration file modified: $(file)
BIND zone data modified: $(file)
BIND named process writing unexpected file: $(output_fields.fd.name)
Suspicious long random-looking DNS query: $(query_name) from $(srcip)
Coordinated DNS recon: Suricata + BIND saw probes from $(srcip)
Possible DNS tampering: zone file modified + sudo from non-routine user
Possible DNS tampering: zone file modified within 60s of sudo to root
PAM session opened (srv-web: suppressed routine)
PAM session closed (srv-web: suppressed routine)
Successful sudo (srv-web: suppressed routine admin)
POSSIBLE WEBSHELL DROP on srv-web: $(file)
Apache config modified on srv-web: $(file)
nginx config modified on srv-web: $(file)
TLS private key modified on srv-web: $(file)
Apache spawned child process on srv-web — possible webshell exec
Apache process reading sensitive file on srv-web
HIGH-CONFIDENCE WEBSHELL DELIVERY on srv-web: drop + access correlated
POSSIBLE REVERSE SHELL on srv-web: Apache shell + outbound connection
Possible DNS tampering: zone file modified within 60s of sudo to root on srv-dns-bind
PAM session opened (srv-sql: suppressed routine)
PAM session closed (srv-sql: suppressed routine)
Successful sudo (srv-sql: suppressed routine admin)
MySQL config modified on srv-sql: $(file)
Per-DB MySQL config modified on srv-sql: $(file)
MySQL credential brute force on srv-sql: 5+ access denied in 60s
mysqld spawned child process on srv-sql — possible SQL RCE
mysqld reading sensitive file on srv-sql — possible compromise
Coordinated MySQL brute force on srv-sql: app-layer + network layer agree
POSSIBLE SQL RCE + EXFIL on srv-sql: mysqld shell + outbound
Windows Logon Success (srv-ad-dns: suppressed routine)
Windows User Logoff (srv-ad-dns: suppressed routine)
Suspicious cmd (srv-ad-dns: suppressed routine admin)
Suspicious cmd execution (srv-ad-dns: suppressed routine)
PowerShell script file in Temp (srv-ad-dns: routine)
PowerShell deleted files (srv-ad-dns: routine admin)
AD brute force on srv-ad-dns: 8+ failed logons in 120s
NewCredentials (Type 9) logon on srv-ad-dns - possible PTH
AD user account created on srv-ad-dns
Possible AS-REP roasting on srv-ad-dns: 5+ Kerberos pre-auth failures in 60s
Non-standard service install on srv-ad-dns: $(win.eventdata.imagePath)
KNOWN CREDENTIAL DUMP TOOL on srv-ad-dns: $(win.eventdata.image)
ACTIVE AD COMPROMISE on srv-ad-dns: brute force + credential dump tool
POSSIBLE PERSISTENCE on srv-ad-dns: new account + non-std service within 5min
OneDrive Explorer access (srv-ftp: suppressed routine)
taskschd.dll loaded (srv-ftp: suppressed routine)
BT user service registration (srv-ftp: suppressed routine)
Per-user system service registration (srv-ftp: suppressed)
WinRM loopback (srv-ftp: suppressed loopback)
System time changed (srv-ftp: routine NTP)
Software protection service (srv-ftp: suppressed routine)
CIS benchmark password history (srv-ftp: install state, suppressed)
PowerShell deleted files (srv-ftp: routine)
PowerShell created exe in Windows root (srv-ftp: routine installer)
Discovery activity (srv-ftp: Defender benign)
FTP/RDP/Windows brute force on srv-ftp: 5+ failed logons in 60s
POSSIBLE HTTP WEBSHELL DROP on srv-ftp: $(file)
FTP file uploaded on srv-ftp: $(file)
File create under /inetpub on srv-ftp by Sysmon EID 11 - check process
IIS service stopped on srv-ftp - possible defense evasion
IIS applicationHost.config modified on srv-ftp
SUCCESSFUL BRUTE FORCE on srv-ftp: brute force + successful logon
HIGH-CONFIDENCE HTTP WEBSHELL DELIVERY on srv-ftp
AD user account CREATED on srv-ad-dns: $(win.eventdata.targetUserName)
AD user account enabled on srv-ad-dns: $(win.eventdata.targetUserName)
AD admin-initiated password reset on srv-ad-dns: target=$(win.eventdata.targetUserName)
AD routine account event on srv-ad-dns (downgraded)
SCENARIO: Coordinated DNS recon — 3+ AXFR attempts from $(srcip) in 5min
SCENARIO: Web reconnaissance — 5+ 4xx errors from $(srcip) in 2min
SCENARIO: Web exploitation probing — 3+ 5xx errors from $(srcip) in 60s
SCENARIO: SSH brute force campaign — 10+ failed logins from $(srcip) in 2min
SCENARIO: AD brute force campaign — 10+ Windows auth failures in 3min
SCENARIO: Recon → exploit chain — DNS recon then web 5xx from $(srcip)
SCENARIO: Multi-stage webshell deployment — 2+ webshell drops in 10min
SCENARIO: Webshell delivery — file drop followed by activity within 60s
SCENARIO: Sustained MySQL brute force — multiple bursts in 2min
SCENARIO: Sustained FTP brute force — multiple bursts in 3min
SCENARIO: Active web server compromise — Apache spawned 2+ shells in 5min
SCENARIO: CRITICAL INCIDENT — credential dumping tool executed on DC
SCENARIO: Persistence campaign — 3+ AD accounts created in 10min
Windows Logon Success (suppressed by SENTINEL-AI Phase 1)
Windows User Logoff (suppressed by SENTINEL-AI Phase 1)
DoH query via dnsdist (base)
DoH query from non-loopback client: $(doh_client_ip) -> $(doh_query)
DoH query with abnormally long label (exfil candidate): $(doh_query)
DoH suspicious record type query: $(doh_qtype) for $(doh_query)
High-volume DoH from $(doh_client_ip): >30 queries in 60s (exfil pattern)
DoH exfiltration confirmed: 5+ long-label queries from $(doh_client_ip) in 60s
Suricata TLS metadata (base)
L1: TLS to DoH endpoint from $(src_ip) (SNI: $(tls.sni))
L1 HIGH: External TLS to DoH endpoint from $(src_ip) (SNI: $(tls.sni))

rule.field: SENTINEL DoH access from non-DMZ client
rule.description: L2: External client hit DoH endpoint (Falco syscall view) - $(output)
L3+L4 correlation: DoH query from $(doh_client_ip) reached BIND forwarder
L1 confirmed: Multiple external DoH TLS connections from $(src_ip)
L1+L2 cross-source: Suricata network and Falco syscall both confirm external DoH access
4-LAYER CONFIRMED DoH abuse: Suricata+Falco+dnsdist+BIND all observed external client
DOH EXFILTRATION CONFIRMED: Sustained high-entropy query burst from $(doh_client_ip), externally sourced
Suppress: Ansible WinRM temp file creation (not malware)
Suppress: Ansible WinRM base64 encoded PowerShell cleanup (not malware)
Suppress: WinRM provider host file writes (not malware)
Suppress: Ansible GUID temp dir file drop (not malware)
Suppress: WinRM-spawned encoded PowerShell (Ansible module wrapper, not malware)
Suppress: PowerShell execution policy test file (not malware)
Suppress: Built-in system account logon failure (not compromised user)
Suppress: Machine account authentication failure (not brute force)
Suppress: Loopback/local RDP authentication failure (not brute force)
AD-CS: Certificate request received by CA
AD-CS: Certificate issued by CA
AD-CS: Certificate request denied by CA
AD-CS: Certificate request received — requester=$(win.eventdata.requester) template=$(win.eventdata.attributes)
AD-CS: Certificate request on vulnerable ESC1 template — requester=$(win.eventdata.requester)
AD-CS: Certificate issued by CA — requester=$(win.eventdata.requester)
AD-CS: Certificate ISSUED on vulnerable ESC1 template — ESC1 abuse confirmed, requester=$(win.eventdata.requester)
AD-CS: Certificate request DENIED on vulnerable ESC1 template — attacker probing, requester=$(win.eventdata.requester)
AD-CS: ESC1 UPN override detected — attacker supplied custom SAN:upn, requester=$(win.eventdata.requester) attributes=$(win.eventdata.attributes)
AD-CS: Known certificate abuse tool executed — image=$(win.eventdata.image) cmdline=$(win.eventdata.commandLine)
AD-CS: PowerShell certificate abuse tool keywords detected in script block
AD-CS: Burst of denied cert requests on ESC1 template — automated tool likely, requester=$(win.eventdata.requester)
AD-CS: ESC1 CONFIRMED — multiple UPN-override cert requests from same requester, possible domain privilege escalation via certificate abuse
nginx TLS access on srv-web (protocol=$(tls_protocol) cipher=$(tls_cipher))
Weak TLS: non-PFS cipher on srv-web from $(srcip) — RSA key exchange has no forward secrecy (PCI-DSS 4.2.1)
SENTINEL: Repeated non-PFS TLS connections to srv-web from $(srcip) — possible TLS audit scan or persistent misconfiguration
MySQL query logged on srv-sql
CRITICAL: MySQL query targeting infra_credentials table on srv-sql — possible credential theft
SENTINEL: Repeated queries against infra_credentials — bulk credential dump in progress
LATERAL MOVEMENT: External NTLM logon to DC from $(win.eventdata.ipAddress) as $(win.eventdata.targetUserName)
Act 3: AS-REP Roast attempt detected via Suricata
Act 3: AS-REP Roast CAMPAIGN — repeated Kerberos AS-REQ with no pre-auth
Act 3: Kerberoast — SPN service ticket requested for $(win.eventdata.serviceName)
Act 3: Kerberoast CAMPAIGN — bulk SPN ticket requests detected
Act 3: SSH Lateral Movement — successful SSH login from attacker VLAN $(srcip)
Act 3: Raw TCP exfiltration to attacker host $(data.dest_ip):$(data.dest_port)
Act 3: Data exfiltration CONFIRMED — staging bundle transferred to $(data.dest_ip)
Act 3: Symbolic ransom note detected at $(syscheck.path)
